assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_029",
  "rule_id": "Set GNOME3 Screensaver Inactivity Timeout",
  "category": "safe_to_remediate",
  "confidence": 0.96,
  "rationale": "The remediation only modifies DConf configuration files under /etc/dconf/db/local.d, which does not affect system boot, networking, or authentication mechanisms. The change is limited to desktop session timeout and can be applied without risking loss of SSH or console access.",
  "risk_factors": [
    "Desktop user experience impact",
    "Requires GNOME3 environment to be present"
  ],
  "safe_next_steps": [
    "Create or edit /etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-security-settings with content:",
    "[org/gnome/desktop/session]",
    "idle-delay=uint32 900",
    "Add the corresponding lock file entry in /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks",
    "Run `dconf update` to apply changes",
    "Verify the setting with `dconf read /org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay`"
  ],
  "requires_reboot": false,
  "touches_authn_authz": false,
  "touches_networking": false,
  "touches_filesystems": true
}
assistant_message.reasoning:
We need classify. Finding is about GNOME3 screensaver inactivity timeout. Changing DConf config. This is a desktop environment setting. Typically safe, no lockout of SSH. It affects user auth? touches authn? No, touches filesystems (writes to /etc/dconf...). Not networking. Not auth. So classification: safe_to_remediate.

Confidence high maybe 0.95. Provide rationale.

Provide risk_factors: maybe "Desktop environment change", "Potential user impact". safe_next_steps: "Create/modify /etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-security-settings with idle-delay=uint32 900", "Lock setting in /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks", "Run dconf update". requires_reboot? Dconf changes may require logout/relogin; not reboot. So false.

touches_authn_authz: false. touches_networking: false. touches_filesystems: true (since writes to files).

Return JSON.
